calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Re Amended Petition Of Ernest Lentfer To Perpetuate Testimony Of Minor Lentfer By Videotaped Deposition
Off Calendar Aug-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christopher Hersom, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for petitioner / moving party Ernest Lentfer
T. Eric Sun, Esq., of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / opposing party Copeland Corporation LLC
Daniel L. Martens, Esq., of DAL SAGLIO & MARTENS LLP (310-662-4748), appearing via CourtCall for defendant / opposing party Aeroject-General Corporation

RULING:
PETITIONER ERNEST LENTFER'S PETITION TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF MINOR LENTFER BY VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Ernest Lentfer's amended petition to perpetuate testimony of Minor Lentfer by videotaped deposition is denied. The supplemental papers fail to address the issues raised at the prior hearing. Specifically, petitioner fails to set forth facts why any of the parties expected to be named as defendants in the anticipated survival and wrongful death action were not named as defendants in Minor Lentfer's personal injury lawsuit. Nor does petitioner state as to any of the parties expected to be named as defendants in the anticipated survival and wrongful death lawsuit when it was determined that Minor Lentfer had relevant testimony to provide as to those parties or whether any of those parties were mentioned in discovery responses served by Minor Lentfer in his personal injury lawsuit so that the defendants who were named in that lawsuit could make appropriate Proposition 51 inquiry about them. Absent unusual circumstances, it is a misuse of scarce judicial resources and contrary to the purposes underlying Proposition 51 to permit trial preservation testimony of a person after his own asbestos personal injury lawsuit has been resolved. No such unusual circumstances have been shown here. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)

Shawn M. Ridley, Esq., of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY, PLLP (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / opposing party Eaton

RULING:
PETITIONER ERNEST LENTFER'S AMENDED PETITION OF ERNEST LENTFER TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF MINOR LENTFER BY VIDEOTAPED DEPOSITION

Argued and the Court continued the hearing on Ernest Lentfer's amended petition to perpetuate testimony of Minor Lentfer by videotaped deposition to August 30, 2011 at 9 am in Department 220 for submission of supplemental briefings. Supplemental petition due by 8/19, supplemental opposition due by 8/24 and Supplemental reply due by 8/26. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not Reported. = (220/HEK)

Zachary M. Lewis, Esq., of DAL SAGLIO & MARTENS LLP (310-563-2171), appearing via CourtCall for defendant / opposing party Aerojet-General Corporation

RULING:
PETITIONER ERNEST LENTFER'S PETITION TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF MINOR LENTFER BY VIDEOTAPED ORAL DEPOSITION

Argued. The Court sustained defendants' objections, petitioner has 15 days to amend the petition. Counsel for defendant Aerojet is to prepare an order for the Court's signature. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported = (220/HEK)
